question_text: A mobile phone receives signals using radio waves. Explain why radio waves are suitable for this use and name one danger associated with prolonged exposure to radio waves from mobile phones.
correct_answer: Radio waves are suitable because they can travel long distances and penetrate buildings/obstacles to reach the phone. One danger is that prolonged exposure may cause heating of body tissues or potential health effects, though this is still being researched.
explanation: Radio waves have long wavelengths so they diffract around obstacles and travel far, making them ideal for wireless communication. While radio waves are non-ionizing and less energetic than UV or X-rays, there is ongoing research about whether prolonged mobile phone use near the head could have health effects.
